vitals.terrain: volcanic roads, sea-to-summit climbs, mountains, coastal paths
vitals.aid_stations: self-supported; supermarkets, gas stations for resupply
vitals.time_limit: flexible Audax format with ferry timing constraints

course_description.overview: Self-supported ultra-distance road Audax spanning five Canary Islands: Lanzarote, Fuerteventura, Gran Canaria, Tenerife, and La Gomera. Riders connect via ferries, following iconic roads with epic climbs through volcanic landscapes, from beaches to mountains.

key_segments.name: Lanzarote
key_segments.description: Fast start with 1,710 m elevation to first ferry.

key_segments.name: Fuerteventura
key_segments.description: Endless climbs, rough asphalt, night riding to next ferry.

key_segments.name: Gran Canaria
key_segments.description: New route in 2026 with iconic roads and epic climbs.

key_segments.name: Tenerife
key_segments.description: Critical 3,487 m climb over Mount Teide to catch ferry, long descent.

key_segments.name: La Gomera
key_segments.description: Final competitive loop with 3,275 m elevation after mandatory rest.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Ferry timing creates pressure; massive elevation after 500+ km fatigues riders. Drafting allowed fosters community until final island race.

logistics.getting_there: Fly to Lanzarote Airport (ACE); start from there. Ferries connect islands (Lanzarote-Fuerteventura-Gran Canaria-Tenerife-La Gomera).
logistics.accommodation: Self-supported; some book hotels for rest (e.g., Tenerife). Final La Gomera has mandatory overnight for fast riders.
logistics.registration_details: Via Outer Detours; limited field, early signup advised for 5th edition.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bike suitable; strip non-importants for final stage. Low gearing recommended for climbs.
logistics.local_considerations: Warm winter temps; variable weather (cold Teide descent, wind). Spanish ferries require timing; supermarkets for food.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic island-hopping Audax on volcanic roads with ferry chases and Teide summit.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Ultra road riders seeking adventure, community drafting, and winter sun in extreme terrain.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for unique Canary scenery and format blending Audax pace with final race.
pro_tips: Nail ferry timings—aim for Audax pace to group start on La Gomera.
Pack for cold Teide descent despite warm start.
Refuel at supermarkets; carry cash for ferries.
Train climbs; 3,500m+ days after 500km demand fitness.
